content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach consistently names what they're noticing before asking questions, and the reads are specific rather than generic. Turn 2 names the laugh as carrying knowing ('there's something in it'). Turn 4 names a precise somatic/gestural read — the dismissive wave — at the exact moment it happened, which catches the client and shifts the conversation. Turn 6 reads beneath the 'boring' framing to the defended-against feeling. Turn 8 explicitly names the explanation-as-defense ('That's a very good explanation') before going underneath it — this is reading defensiveness-as-identity-signal in action. Turn 10 mirrors back her own words to surface what she's avoiding. Turn 12 quotes her exact phrase ('If I was just good enough at it') — a linguistic-caveat read that lands. Reads precede questions throughout, they are timely (in the moment, not after), and they track the rehearsed/analytical stance the client keeps retreating into. The coach is reading the defended performance, not just the content.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the laugh and what it carries ('you already know the answer is going to be uncomfortable') — read precedes question."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Specific gestural read — the dismissive wave — named in the moment it occurred. Client visibly caught."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads underneath the 'boring' frame to the avoided feeling. Implicit read of the defense, though not explicitly named."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the explanation itself as a move ('That's a very good explanation') — reads defensiveness-as-identity-signal without colluding with it."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects her exact words back, surfacing the asymmetry between what she was doing and what she wanted."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Linguistic-caveat read — quotes 'If I was just good enough at it' and uses it as the entry point. Read precedes question."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Good question but no explicit read named here; relies on the prior thread."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["defensiveness-as-identity-signal"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["looking-for-whats-wrong"],
  "notes": "Reads are specific, timely, and grounded in observable behavior (gestures, laughs, exact phrasing). The coach repeatedly distinguishes the said from the shown."
}
